---
title: "Android Client"
description-meta: "Install and use the openme Android app — download, profile setup, and sending knocks."
keywords: [openme Android, openme knock Android, SPA Android app, openme APK]
---

The openme Android app lets you send knock packets from your Android phone or
tablet. Add profiles manually or scan the QR code generated by the server.

---

## Download {#download}

[Download for Android →](../download/android.qmd)

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Google Play / APK listing -->
![openme on Google Play](../images/placeholder-android-play.png){.placeholder}

---

## Add a Profile {#profile}

1. Open **openme** on your device.
2. Tap **Add profile** (or the **+** button).
3. Enter the profile details, or tap **Scan QR** and scan the QR code
   generated by `openme add --qr <name>` on the server.

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Add profile screen -->
![Add profile screen](../images/placeholder-android-add-profile.png){.placeholder}

| Field | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| Name | Friendly label for this server (e.g. "Home Server") |
| Server | Hostname or IP of the server |
| Port | UDP knock port (default: `54154`) |
| Server public key | Curve25519 public key — from `openme add` output |
| Your private key | Ed25519 private key — generated by `openme add` |

---

## Send a Knock {#knock}

1. Select the profile in the profile list.
2. Tap **Knock**.
3. A brief confirmation toast confirms the packet was sent.

<!-- Screenshot placeholder: Main screen with Knock button -->
![Profile list and Knock button](../images/placeholder-android-knock.png){.placeholder}

::: {.callout-note}
The app sends a UDP packet and does not wait for a response — the server never
replies. A "sent" confirmation means the packet left the device; it does not
confirm the knock was accepted.
:::
